docs.text: Home Introduction Hello, this site serves as an outlet for me to release some pent-up creative (and sometimes useless energy). I like to tinker in numerous things and hopefully someone will find my posts and notes helpful. It's mostly technical but will also be occasionally be hobby related. My posts are broken into two types, blogs and documentation. Documentation will be broken into relevant collections. They should all be in the above navigation links. About Me I’m a seasoned software architect with over 20 years of progressive experience in ERP systems and technology. After graduating from UCLA, I began my career in technology which has led me to the good fortune of working with amazing organizations and professionals, both here and abroad. At my current position as a contractor for one of the largest retail company in the world, we designed and implemented an inventory management event system that processes over 2.5 millions events a day. To reach me, click the lower right to hit one of my social media.

docs.text: Dominoes Introduction My friends and I have been looking for a good online dominoes game to keep us occupy during quarantine, but we couldn't find any. So I'm creating a pet project. It also serves the following purposes. Allows me to design and develop for fun Uses the latest .NET Core technologies Keeps me updated in interesting software methodology Keeps me updated in interesting technology Keep the cost low Production grade code Allows me to keep a handy toolkit in one place. Dominoes have many variations and the one I play the most is called All Fives . A variation to it is we don't play teams (we wouldn't be able to stop cheating). I fully believe quarantine will be over and I'll had over the code to my grand-kids before I'll ever get it done. Software Architecture One of the main purpose of this project to better define and document my software architecture style. This includes the following. Architecture Structure Architecture characteristics Architecture decisions Design principles Technology These are a few technologies I want to implement and use. Programming Language/Framework. Example is .NET Core IDE. Example is Rider Runs on local hardware. An example is Raspberry Pi Applications management. Example is Kubernetes . Repository. Example is GitHub . CI/CD. An example is GitLab CI/CD Event streaming technology. An example is Kafka ] Datastore. An example is PostgreSQL External Logging System. An example is Splunk APM. An example would be New Relic Code Analysis Software. An example is Sonarqube Conclusion I'll have blog posts on the various software architecture and technology. I'll mix and match the conversation.

docs.text: IntelliJ IDEA / Gradle and JDK versions Introduction I joined a new team in my workplace last week and they're running a Java/SpringBoot/Gradle setup. I have no experience in Gradle (and my Java skills aren't the best), so I created a new Gradle project in IntelliJ to play around with. This was the setup. Windows 10 Laptop IntellJ Gradle (IntellJ installed version 6.1.1 in ~\.gradle\wrapper\dists ) openjdk-14 The wizard went smoothly but the build failed with a vague error message Could not initialize class org.codehaus.groovy.vmplugin.v7.Java7 . The wizard didn't generate the expected directories and files and was missing needed files like gradle-wrapper.properties . After much searching and random testing I found the issue. Gradle 6.1.1 requires a lower JDK version than 14. I installed JDK 11 and update the Gradle setting to use it. Delete the old project, create a new one, and it worked! Additional Resources IntelliJ Getting Started with Gradle
docs.title: 2020-06-29 Gradle JDK version mismatch
IntelliJ IDEA / Gradle and JDK versions

docs.text: Libraries Introduction This is a running list of libraries I use heavily in my projects. See my blog post for reasons why. Library Project Type Purpose Automapper All Consolidate/automate mapping code CommandLineParser Console App Standard way to accept command line arguments FluentValidation All Build strongly-typed validation rule MediatR All Simple mediator implementation Serilog All Structured Logging

docs.text: Containerized Web API and accessing Azure Key Vault Context Azure Web App Service allows you to deploy a containerized Web API. This is useful because there's many more ways to host a container app than a .NET Web API; and at some point I may want to try them out. Creating a docker container is easy; just the follow the links below. Links Dockerize an existing ASP.NET Core application Microsoft Dotnet Docker tutorial repository Gotchas There was one major gotcha that took me a while to resolve. Azure Managed Identity doesn't work inside a local Docker container This means we can't authenticate to Azure Key Vault using Azure Managed Identity. Here's more discussion and workarounds. The way to get around it is to provide the app with your Azure Token. You'll need to the following. Install Azure CLI and jq Log into azure : az login Get your key vault access token: az account get-access-token --resource=https://vault.azure.net | jq -r .accessToken Add the token somewhere so your app can use it. I added the token in appsettings.Development.json because it's only needed for development. In production, the container will run in Azure Web App Service and it'll be able to authenticate through MSI. Implement reading the token and using it to create the KeyVaultClient . Here's a sample code. var accessToken = builtConfig [ "AZURE_KEY_VAULT_ACCESS_TOKEN" ]; if (! string . IsNullOrEmpty ( accessToken )) { logger . Debug ( "Using Access Token from Environment variable to access Key Vault" ); keyVaultClient = new KeyVaultClient (( string _ , string __ , string ___ ) => Task . FromResult ( accessToken )); } Now that the file appsettings.Development.json contains sensitive information, I removed the file from being tracked in git.
docs.title: 2020-06-23 Docker and Azure KeyVault
Containerized Web API and accessing Azure Key Vault
